entry.title: Barchart races
entry.content:
Trendy charts

What with global climate disaster and COVID, the bar chart race has become a ubiquitous harbinger of doom for spelling out, in this the age of denial, the stark challenges facing us.

This demo uses vanillajs to fetch and graph grouped/ordered data by rendering an svg. Most graphing libraries like d3.js and chart.js can do likewise. It can be dropped in any html as a widget as I was aiming for a generic as possible component. I’ve used a raw data set here and excluded results less than 1000. It’s easy enough to adjust the width, the viewport, the speed of the run. The fact the data is ordered makes the logic much simpler. It is not the job of the client to clean and sort data although obviously it could.

Todo:

  1. add x and y axes
  2. stop, play, forward buttons
  3. drill downs for each bar
  4. reactjs version
  5. more races!

CO2 emissions

entry.title: Spooky
entry.content:
All dressed up

Hackers 25th Anniversary provides the best excuse for dressing up and not going out. I don’t know if zoom masked balls are a thing this year or whether it is even appropriate to wear masks on a zoom call seeing as how we are pretty much sick of them by now. With not a hoodie in sight the avant-garde fashion on display in the movie seems to have aged well even if us old hackers have not.

Spooky all year round

Not that hackers had much of a problem blending in at Halloween up to now. There have been many changes for the better in the intervening years and the community is much more diverse than before. Many people who identify as hackers today - information security professionals - are more likely to be seen wearing a suit than a dark hoodie. Contrary to the myth it was ever thus. I only acquired my first dark hoodie last year as part of some CTF swag.

entry.title: Revil dread
entry.content:
Are REvil feeling the heat?

REvil gang spokesperson Unkn or Unknown wistfully accepts that travel outside Russia could be very dangerous for them.

Indeed what is the point you would think of turning over hundreds of millions of dollars in monero annually if you are limited to the toxic gangster circles of Moscow and St Petersberg?

Too great a show of wealth in Russia could itself draw unwanted attention from less successful but powerfully placed banditi. When you live in a fascist regime where extra-judicial killings are the norm rather than the exception a healthy bank balance does not come with the normal feeling of security it should.

Stories like those of Eugene Nikulin only reinforce the notion that despite multi-millionaire status it could all come crashing down in an instant, as they observe darkly, alive but in the hands of the FBI or dead at the hands of their competitors or the regime. Indeed the Kremlin’s eye is never far from their operations and paranoia that some gang members are FSB nannies abide.

RaaS - Ransomware as a Service

Despite the huge success of their operation due to the apparent complete inability of Western companies and institutions to secure their data from even run-of-the-mill consumer exploits, the overworked gang are constantly seeking to evolve by copying new techniques from other gangs and to adapt their business model, expanding their RaaS - or Ransomware as a Service so they can pivot to selling tooling and training to other gangs. This outsourcing of work will no doubt reap dividends where they simply do not have enough time in the day to exploit all the targets available to them and also to allow them to put some distance between themselves and the heat.

Perhaps even they (and their girlfriends) might dream that if their operational security has been good enough they might even be able to risk travel abroad in order to spend some of their ill-gotten gains in Paris, Milan, London or New York.

No doubt Western intelligence services and law enforcement would love them to try being as they are currently out of their reach.

Watch the video from Russian OSINT on youtube here.
